display.purpose: Hero h1 and major section openers.

impact.purpose: Cinematic full-bleed dark-section headlines only. World-of-movement, audience-split.

headline.purpose: Section h2 across all light sections.

body.purpose: All paragraph copy. Cap line length 65-75ch.

label.purpose: Eyebrow labels, badges, section markers, breadcrumbs. Captain's-hand annotation voice.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Event card hover lift. Lime glow is the AltCrew signature.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Hero word stagger, parallax photo zoom, motion entrances.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Rust pill CTA. The signature accent action. Hero, headers, conversion moments.

components.name: Crew CTA
components.description: Lime pill on dark surfaces. Audience-split crew side. The 'join' signal.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Outline button. Secondary nav links, organizer alt-CTA.

components.name: Eyebrow Label
components.description: Mono uppercase pill. Section marker, captain's-hand annotation. Always 11px Geist Mono tracked-widest.

components.name: Event Tag
components.description: Solid color-coded pill on event cards. Color communicates category.

components.name: Event Card
components.description: Fixed-size event card with photo, gradient overlay, tag, and title. The world-of-movement carousel building block.
components.html:
Running

Sunset Run Club

components.name: Portrait Card
components.description: Hero portrait card. 18px radius, hairline border, full-bleed image with inset title block.

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Honest text input. Cream background, ground text, 10px radius.

components.name: Mono Section Marker
components.description: Standalone uppercase tracked-widest label. Used as section eyebrow without a pill.

narrative.overview: AltCrew looks the way a club captain's training journal feels. Cream paper, rust ink, hi-vis lime and festival flags. Annotated, not designed. Specific, not staged. Two registers cohabit: the base layer is earthy and quiet (terracotta rust on warm cream, stone neutrals, hand-mono labels in tracked uppercase) where copy lives and trust is built. The event layer is vivid and loud (gold, orange, lime, cyan, hot pink, vivid purple) pulled from the Vizag fitness festival identity. Cards, tags, animated meshes, signal moments. Energy lives there, not throughout. This system rejects the Indian app aesthetic outright: no CRED gradients, no Cult.fit feature stacks, no Bollywood orange-and-gold maximalism, no glassmorphism, no neon-on-black.
keyCharacteristics: Warm cream + terracotta rust + stone neutrals as the resting state
Hi-vis lime + festival flag accents as event-energy signals
Geist Sans for everything except mono-label workhorse and Figtree-900 impact moments
Specific, voiced copy. Sentence fragments OK. Em dashes prohibited.
Photography over UI. Real training over staged stock.

rules.name: The Two-Tier Rule
rules.body: Earthy neutrals carry the page. Festival accents carry the energy. The two tiers do not blur together.

rules.name: The One-Vest Rule
rules.body: Lime is a safety vest, not a wallpaper. Lime fills cover at most one surface in any viewport.

rules.name: The Anti-Gradient Rule
rules.body: No background gradients on text containers. No background-clip: text. The conic event-bar functions as a flag, not decoration.

rules.name: The Mono-Label Rule
rules.body: Eyebrow labels, badges, and section markers are always Geist Mono, 11px, uppercase, tracked-widest.

rules.name: The No-Em-Dash Rule
rules.body: Em dashes are prohibited in copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses. Also no double-hyphens.

rules.name: The 14-Character Headline Rule
rules.body: Audience-split and impact headlines wrap at 14 characters max-width. Longer headlines belong in display, not impact.

rules.name: The Flat-Until-Hover Rule
rules.body: Surfaces have no shadow at rest. Shadows appear only as a state response.

rules.name: The Lime-Tinted Shadow Rule
rules.body: When a shadow is required, it carries a lime tint. Pure black shadows look stale and corporate.

dos: Do use Rust (#e5572b) as the primary accent. Reserve it. If it appears more than twice in a fold, remove one.
Do use Lime Vivid (#c7ff57) only on the crew CTA, success markers, and event tags. One-vest rule.
Do use Geist Mono 11px tracked-widest uppercase for every eyebrow label, badge, and section marker.
Do keep body copy at 17px line-height 1.55, line-length 65-75ch.
Do use full-pill buttons (border-radius: 9999px). Never partial radii.
Do use real city names (Vizag, Hyderabad, Bangalore, Mumbai). Specificity is the brand.
Do use candid training photography. Real people, mid-action, off-center compositions.
Do lift event cards on hover with a lime-tinted shadow. The glow is the AltCrew signature.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion: reduce on every animated surface.
Do keep touch targets at 48x48px minimum.
donts: Don't use CRED-style heavy gradients, neon glow, or gradient text (background-clip: text is forbidden).
Don't stack five Cult.fit-style festival banners above the fold. One festival bar at a time.
Don't use generic Bollywood orange-and-gold maximalism. Rust is terracotta, not cricket-jersey orange.
Don't use glassmorphism, decorative blurs, or backdrop-filter as a default.
Don't clone Strava (black + neon graph) or Whoop (data-forward dark). AltCrew is human-first.
Don't use stock photography of laughing models in pristine athleisure.
Don't write 'We're revolutionising your fitness journey' or any startup-speak phrase.
Don't use em dashes or double-hyphens in copy.
Don't mix two tier-3 festival colors on the same surface.
Don't drop ambient pure-black shadows under cards. Always lime-tinted, always state-driven.
Don't use side-stripe borders as accents. Full borders, background tints, or nothing.
Don't invent new pill shapes or partial radii.
